 {"id":"bd-3czr","title":"demo_showcase tests: live-mode smoke (forced terminal)","description":"Add an e2e test that runs a short live-mode demo with --force-terminal and verifies clean start/stop (bounded output).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:15:32.595139460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:07:38.733449829Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:07:38.733315068Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","live","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-3fph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-3czr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Test intent

We can't fully validate “looks great in a real TTY” in CI, but we *can* validate that live mode:
- starts successfully
- runs for a bounded amount of time
- stops cleanly (no panic)

## Approach

- Run with `--force-terminal --live --no-screen --quick --speed <fast>`
- Keep duration bounded (e.g. runner has an internal max tick count in non-interactive contexts, or test passes a `--max-frames` style flag if we add one)
- Assert exit 0 and output is within an upper bound

## Dependencies

This requires the live driver to exist (`bd-3fph`) and the scene runner (`bd-1jk8`).

 {"id":"bd-3d0j","title":"Markdown parity: render links/images as OSC8 hyperlinks (no URL suffix)","description":"Python Rich 13.9.4 Markdown renders links and images as OSC8 hyperlinks (default `hyperlinks=true`) without appending the URL text.

Acceptance criteria:
- For Markdown links: `[text](url)` renders as just `text` in plain output; ANSI output wraps `text` in OSC8 sequences pointing to `url`.
- For Markdown images: `![alt](url)` renders as `🌆 alt` (image emoji + space + alt text) in plain output; ANSI output wraps `alt` in OSC8 pointing to `url`.
- Provide a Rust API equivalent to Python `Markdown(..., hyperlinks: bool)`; when disabled, render without OSC8.
- Add conformance fixtures: `markdown/link`, `markdown/image`.

Notes:
- Current rich_rust behavior appends ` (url)` to link text; remove for parity.
- Reuse existing OSC8 support via `Style::link(...)`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:12:12.702065296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:32:53.679195213Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:32:53.679132606Z","close_reason":"Completed Markdown parity for links/images: default OSC8 hyperlinks with no URL suffix; hyperlinks=false renders 'text (url)'; images render as emoji + alt text with OSC8 link. Conformance fixtures + e2e tests updated.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["links","markdown","parity"]}

     - conformance fixture kind(s).
3. Enumerate real parity gaps vs Python Rich 13.9.4

   - Behavioral gaps (same API, different output/edge cases).

4. For each gap: create a child bead under bd-3d1t
   - Concrete acceptance criteria.
   - File targets.
   - Conformance fixture plan (when applicable).
   - Dependencies between beads.
5. Re-run `bv --robot-triage` and implement top-impact gaps first
   - After each: add fixtures/tests, run gates, `ubs`, commit+push, close bead, `Sync beads`.

 {"id":"bd-3fe1","title":"Migrate interactive.rs to consistent mutex handling","description":"## Task Description

Migrate mutex lock() calls in `src/interactive.rs` Status struct to use the new sync utility functions.

## Current State Analysis

Status struct has one Mutex-protected field:
```rust
pub struct Status {
    message: Arc<Mutex<String>>,  // Line 30
    live: Option<Live>,
}
```

## Lock Calls to Migrate

| Line | Current Pattern | Context | New Pattern |
|------|-----------------|---------|-------------|
| 40 | `if let Ok(message) = message.lock()` | new() - print fallback | `lock_recover` |
| 67 | `.lock().map(|m| m.clone()).unwrap_or_default()` | new() - render closure | `lock_recover` |
| 83 | `if let Ok(mut slot) = self.message.lock()` | update() | `lock_recover` |

## Migration Pattern

Before (line 83):
```rust
pub fn update(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {
    if let Ok(mut slot) = self.message.lock() {
        *slot = message.into();
    }
    if let Some(live) = &self.live {
        let _ = live.refresh();
    }
}
```

After:
```rust
use crate::sync::lock_recover;

pub fn update(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {
    let mut slot = lock_recover(&self.message);
    *slot = message.into();
    drop(slot);  // Release lock before calling refresh
    if let Some(live) = &self.live {
        let _ = live.refresh();
    }
}
```

## Render Closure Challenge

Line 67 is inside a closure passed to `get_renderable()`:
```rust
let live = Live::with_options(...).get_renderable(move || {
    // ...
    let msg = message_for_render
        .lock()
        .map(|m| m.clone())
        .unwrap_or_default();
    // ...
});
```

This closure needs access to `lock_recover`. Options:
1. Import crate::sync inside the closure (not possible for closures)
2. Use a local helper before the closure
3. Keep as-is since it is unlikely to fail

**Recommendation**: Migrate to:
```rust
let msg = crate::sync::lock_recover(&message_for_render).clone();
```

## Test Code

SharedBuffer in tests uses `.lock().unwrap()` - keep these as-is for test clarity.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-288g (sync module must exist first)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 3 lock() calls in production code migrated
- [ ] Closure access to sync module resolved
- [ ] No behavior changes (pure refactoring)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] cargo clippy passes","notes":"Already migrated - uses crate::sync::lock_recover throughout (5 usages in Status)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:12:23.247356071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:37:58.892764910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:37:58.892612566Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fe1","depends_on_id":"bd-288g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3fe1","depends_on_id":"bd-88kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3q42","title":"BUG: Panel content truncated in emoji_links scene - text cut off at panel edge","description":"## Problem Statement

Panel content is truncated at the panel edge, cutting off text mid-word. This indicates a content measurement or word-wrapping issue.

## Observed Output

```
╭─────────── :bell: [bold]Notification[/] ────────────╮
│ :sparkles: [bold]New Release Available![/] :sparkle │
│                                                     │
│ Version 2.5.0 includes:                             │
│ :white_check_mark: Improved table rendering         │
│ :white_check_mark: New panel styles                 │
│ :white_check_mark: Better Unicode support           │
│                                                     │
│ [dim]View release notes:[/] [cyan underline]github. │  ← TRUNCATED
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The URL `github.com/...` is cut off at `github.`

## Related Issues

- This may share root cause with emoji shortcode issues (those `:bell:` etc should be emojis)
- Panel width calculation may not account for full content width

## Root Cause Analysis

### Hypothesis 1: Panel width is too narrow for content

```rust
// Panel might have explicit width that's too small
Panel::from_text(content).width(50)  // Too narrow
```

### Hypothesis 2: Content measurement doesn't include full text

If markup/emoji processing changes content length, the panel might be sized for raw text but rendered with processed text of different length.

### Hypothesis 3: Word wrapping not applied

Panel should wrap long lines, not truncate:
```rust
// Expected behavior:
│ [dim]View release notes:[/]        │
│ [cyan underline]github.com/...[/]  │
```

## Files to Investigate

- src/renderables/panel.rs - Content measurement, wrapping
- src/text.rs - Text::wrap() method
- src/bin/demo_showcase/emoji_links_scene.rs - Scene content

## Implementation Requirements

### 1. Ensure Panel wraps content

```rust
impl Panel {
    fn render(&self, console: &Console, options: &ConsoleOptions) -> Vec<Segment> {
        let inner_width = self.calculate_inner_width(options);
        
        // CRITICAL: Wrap content to inner width
        let wrapped_content = self.content.wrap(inner_width);
        
        // Then render wrapped content
    }
}
```

### 2. Fix measurement to include all content

```rust
impl RichMeasure for Panel {
    fn rich_measure(&self, console: &Console, options: &ConsoleOptions) -> Measurement {
        // Include FULL content width, including after emoji/markup processing
        let content_width = self.content.cell_width();  // After processing
        // ...
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_panel_wraps_long_content() {
    let console = Console::new();
    let long_text = "This is a very long line that should wrap nicely instead of being truncated at the panel edge.";
    
    let panel = Panel::from_text(long_text).width(40);
    let segments = panel.render(&console, 40);
    let output = segments_to_string(&segments);
    
    // All words should be present
    assert!(output.contains("truncated"), "Content was truncated");
    assert!(output.contains("edge"), "Content was truncated");
    
    // No line should exceed panel width
    for line in output.lines() {
        assert!(line.len() <= 42, "Line exceeds panel width: {}", line);
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_panel_doesnt_truncate_mid_word() {
    let text = "word1 word2 word3 word4 word5";
    let panel = Panel::from_text(text).width(20);
    let output = render_panel_to_string(&panel);
    
    // Should not contain partial words
    assert!(!output.contains("wor "), "Word truncated mid-way");
    assert!(!output.contains("ord"), "Word truncated mid-way");
}
```

### E2E Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_emoji_links_scene_no_truncation() {
    init_test_logging();
    info!("TEST: emoji_links scene content not truncated");
    
    let console = Console::builder().width(100).force_terminal(true).build();
    console.begin_capture();
    
    run_emoji_links_scene(&console, &test_state());
    
    let output = console.export_text();
    
    // Check for specific content that was truncated
    assert!(output.contains("github.com") || output.contains("github"),
        "GitHub link content missing or truncated");
    
    // Check no lines end with partial words (heuristic)
    for line in output.lines() {
        let trimmed = line.trim_end_matches(|c: char| c == '│' || c == ' ');
        if trimmed.ends_with('.') && !trimmed.ends_with("...") {
            // Suspicious - might be truncated URL
            info!(line = trimmed, "Potentially truncated line");
        }
    }
    
    info!("[PASS] No obvious truncation detected");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Panel content wraps instead of truncating
- [ ] Long URLs wrap to next line
- [ ] No mid-word truncation
- [ ] Content measurement accounts for processed text
- [ ] Unit tests verify wrapping behavior
- [ ] E2E test validates emoji_links scene content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:46:57.169017355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:04:24.991296409Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:56:47.256954855Z","close_reason":"Fixed by prior emoji fix: Content now properly processed with emoji::replace and markup::render_or_plain before Panel::from_rich_text, which handles word wrapping correctly","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","panel","truncation"]}

For each scene name (from the registry), run:
- `demo_showcase --scene <name> --no-live --no-screen --no-interactive --quick --width 80 --height 24 --color-system none`

Assertions:
- exits 0 (unless the scene is explicitly intended to demonstrate non-zero exit)
- completes under a tight timeout
- stdout contains a stable marker (scene title/header)

## Logging

Use the harness logs to record duration and output sizes so performance regressions are visible.

## Acceptance criteria

- CI catches “scene hangs” and “scene crashes” immediately.

+{"id":"bd-3r3a","title":"Prompt max_length(0) should clamp to 1 byte","description":"Root cause: Prompt/Select/Confirm builder methods accept max_length=0, but read_line_limited then rejects even newline/default path with InputTooLong, making prompts unusable. Fix: clamp max_length to at least 1 for all interactive prompt builders and add regression tests for prompt/select/confirm zero-length configuration behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:27:51.576018280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:30:34.314932728Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T07:30:34.314871464Z","close_reason":"Completed: clamp max_length zero to one with regressions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["input-validation","interactive","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3r3a","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:27:51.592495538Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rhg","title":"demo_showcase: interactive helpers integration","description":"Use Status/Prompt/Pager to make the demo feel like a real CLI wizard when running in a TTY.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.668932689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:21.872054619Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:21.871927131Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","interactive"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the showcase feel like a real operator tool, not just output. Use the new interactive helpers *only when safe* (TTY):

- **Prompt**: ask for scenario / speed / whether to export
- **Status**: spinner while “preparing deployment plan” / “running checks”
- **Pager**: show long Markdown (runbook/release notes) without dumping everything into scrollback

## Safety

- If `Console::is_terminal()` is false, Prompt/Pager must not block.
- Provide `--no-interactive` to disable even in a TTY (useful for scripts).

## Acceptance criteria

- Interactive mode improves UX but is never required to run the demo.
- `--no-interactive` yields deterministic non-blocking output.

 {"id":"bd-3s5n","title":"demo_showcase: CLI completeness pass (seed + links)","description":"Complete the CLI contract: add missing flags (--seed, --links/--no-links, etc.) and align --help output with the finalized spec.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:46.788764478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:19.158199409Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:19.158139518Z","close_reason":"Completed: add --seed + --links flags (help/tests/README)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3s5n","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-3s5n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

Early scaffolding landed a basic parser, but the *final* demo experience needs a few additional flags to support determinism + UX parity with the spec.

## Required additions

- `--seed <u64>`
  - used to seed any pseudo-random demo data (service IDs, jitter, stage durations)
  - default should be stable (e.g. 0 or fixed constant) so `cargo test` snapshots don't drift

- `--links` / `--no-links`
  - control OSC8 hyperlink emission independent of other styling

- Ensure `--help` and `--list-scenes` reflect the actual supported flags and scenes.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--seed` parses and rejects invalid values (non-integer, negative, etc.).
- Deterministic outputs are stable across runs when the same seed/width/height are used.
- Hyperlink control is explicit and defaults to a sensible value (likely auto-enabled when interactive).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:57Z"}]}
